zh.lifeSummary: 當Rose Barber於1503年出生在英國素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可時，她的父親Sir James Barber17歲，母親Lady Alice Bohun13歲。她於大約1524年與Richard Grenling在英國英格蘭素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1559年7月31日在英國素福爾可福雷辛費爾德享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rose Barber于1503年出生在英国素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可时，她的父亲Sir James Barber17岁，母亲Lady Alice Bohun13岁。她于大约1524年与Richard Grenling在英国英格兰素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1559年7月31日在英国素福爾可福雷辛費爾德享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a barber, from Middle English barb(o)ur ‘barber’ (Anglo-Norman French barber, Old French barbier, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). In the Middle Ages barbers not only cut hair and shaved beards, but also practised surgery and pulled teeth.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name from German Barbier ‘barber’ (compare 1 above).
Catalan: occupational name for a barber, barber (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Berber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Salvador, Guillermo, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Luis, Adriana, Alfredo, Angelina, Armando, Carlos.
Catalan: probably a variant of   Barber  . This surname is most common in Mexico.
Turkish: occupational name from berber ‘barber’.

variants.name: Barbero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ricardo, Alvino, Ana, Angel, Arsenio, Fernando, Gustavo, Jaime, Jose, Maribel, Mario, Nenita.  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Domenic, Ernani, Giulio, Italo.  French Emile, Yves.

Spanish and Italian (Piedmont): occupational name for a barber-surgeon, barbero, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’ (from Latin barba); see also Barber . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Barbieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Rocco, Aldo, Cosmo, Dino, Domenic, Geno, Giovanna, Guido.
Italian: occupational name from barbiere ‘barber-surgeon’ (see Barber ), from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbour
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: occupational name from Old French barbeor ‘barber’. Compare the English form Barber .

History: George Barbour sailed from England on the Transport in 1635. He became a freeman of Dedham, MA, in 1647 and settled eventually at Medfield. A Scottish merchant named Barbour settled in eastern VA in the latter half of the 17th century.

variants.name: Borbely
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Antal, Ferenc, Ildiko, Kalman, Laszlo, Miklos.
Hungarian (Borbély): occupational name for a barber, Hungarian borbély. See also Barber .

variants.name: Barbeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Monique, Alcide, Alphonse, Amiee, Armand, Gisele, Jean Francois, Lucien, Lucienne, Michel.
French:

from barbeau ‘barbel’, a type of fish, hence a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, or a nickname for a man with a sparse beard, the fish being distinguished by beardlike growths on either side of its mouth.

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ostensibly from the vocabulary word denoting the flower (Latin rosa). However, the name was in use throughout the Middle Ages, long before any of the other girls' names derived from flowers, which are generally of 19th-century origin. In part it may refer to the flower as a symbol of the Virgin Mary, but it seems more likely that it also has a Germanic origin, probably as a short form of various girls' names based on hros ‘horse’ or hrōd ‘fame’. The Latinate form Rohesia is commonly found in documents of the Middle Ages. As well as being a name in its own right, it is currently used as a short form of Rosemary and, less often (because of their different pronunciation), of other names beginning Ros-, such as Rosalind and Rosamund .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Rose Barber was born in 1503, in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, her father, Sir James Barber, was 17 and her mother, Lady Alice Bohun, was 13. She married Richard Grenling about 1524, in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 31 July 1559, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rose Barber est née en 1503, à Stradbroke, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir James Barber, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice Bohun, avait 13 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Grenling environ 1524, à Stradbroke, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 31 juillet 1559, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rose Barber 1503 in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir James Barber 17 und ihre Mutter Lady Alice Bohun 13. Sie hat um 1524 in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Richard Grenling geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 31. Juli 1559 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rose Barber è nata il nel 1503 nel Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Sir James Barber, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Lady Alice Bohun, aveva 13 anni. Lei sposato Richard Grenling circa 1524, nel Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 31 luglio 1559, nel Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rose Barberが1503年にイギリススフォルクストラドブロクで生まれた時，父Sir James Barberは17歳，母Lady Alice Bohunは13歳であった。約1524年にイギリスイギリススフォルクストラドブロクでRichard Grenlingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1559年7月31日にイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rose Barber(이)가 1503년 영국 수폴크 스트라드브로크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir James Barber(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Alice Bohun(은)는 13세였다. 그녀는 대략 1524년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스트라드브로크에서 Richard Grenling(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1559년 7월 31일 영국 수폴크 프레징필드에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rose Barber nasceu em 1503, em Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir James Barber, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice Bohun, tinha 13 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Grenling aproximadamente 1524, em Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 31 de julho de 1559, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rose Barber родилась в 1503 г. в местности страдброк, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir James Barber было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Bohun, 13. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Grenling около 1524 г. в местности страдброк, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 31 июля 1559 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rose Barber nació en 1503, en Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir James Barber, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Lady Alice Bohun, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Richard Grenling aproximadamente 1524, en Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 31 de julio de 1559, en Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años.
